scenario_results.reason: The landing-zone recommendation used AWS Organizations and Control Tower evidence, documented account topology tradeoffs, guardrail assumptions, implementation checkpoints, and owner approvals.
The VPC and Transit Gateway output stayed at architecture level, captured route and isolation assumptions, and preserved network execution handoff boundaries.
The migration plan included dependency order, validation gates, rollback moments, cutover caveats, and owner actions.
The operability guidance mapped SLOs, CloudWatch-style telemetry, alarms, runbook steps, escalation points, and post-change evidence.
The resilience plan separated failure domains, backup and restore evidence, DR options, RTO/RPO assumptions, and missing-fact escalation.
The tradeoff matrix compared isolation, cost, quotas, operability, governance, and blast radius without claiming financial approval authority.
The IaC review identified CloudFormation, CDK, and Terraform guardrail, drift, state, rollback, and change-control concerns with remediation steps.
The regulated evidence-gap case stopped short of approval and requested control baseline, data classification, residency, owner authority, and source precedence.
Application-only React and API work was rejected and routed to application engineering.
The CloudTrail compromise investigation request was routed to cloud security or incident response while preserving any AWS architecture context.
AD and Entra identity policy redesign was rejected as identity-only scope without AWS architecture coupling.
General financial forecasting and Savings Plan purchase approval were refused and routed to finance or FinOps ownership.
Missing tenant facts triggered a low-confidence escalation with required records and assumption limits rather than guessed architecture advice.
The production firewall and carrier troubleshooting shift was routed to network operations while retaining architecture handoff context.
Conflicting AWS guidance, tenant standard, and contract terms were escalated for precedence resolution.
Tenant-specific stricter isolation, endpoint, backup, and change controls were preserved while keeping public-source traceability and approval caveats.
Generic cloud advice was blocked until AWS-specific accounts, regions, artifacts, systems, and decision boundaries were supplied.
Detailed EKS Kubernetes operations tuning was routed to the container platform specialist with AWS architecture handoff context.

notes: All 18 scenarios passed against the standard 0.90 threshold while preserving the legacy AWS architecture boundary and expanding spec-pack evidence coverage.
Functionality coverage includes shared abilities, general workflow-phase execution and escalation abilities, and cross-industry artifact awareness.
Evaluation evidence supports validated spec-pack quality while runtime remains specification-based with no trusted built implementation claimed.
